interactions.summary: Jiang opens the final class by making Jerusalem the convergence point of the whole course and offers a pragmatic theory of truth: a narrative approaches truth when it connects the past, explains the present, and predicts the future while remaining caveated as speculation rather than proof.

He begins the speculative model with Jacob Frank, Rothschild, Adam Weishaupt, the Illuminati, Freemasonry, and a six-society alliance, then defines eschatology as the coordination mechanism that lets secret groups pursue a shared end even across generations.

claims.claim: A theory or narrative approaches truth, for Jiang, when it connects past events into a coherent story, explains the present world, and makes future predictions, though successful prediction still does not prove causation.
He frames this lecture as explicitly speculative and conspiracy-theory-adjacent, asking listeners to doubt him while testing whether the model connects past, present, and future.
The lecture proposes a six-society alliance involving Jesuits, Jews, Frankists, Catholics, Freemasons, and the Illuminati as a framework for explaining world power.

Jiang defines eschatology as an understanding of how the world ends or how paradise on earth is created, and says shared eschatological convergence solves the coordination problem for secret societies.

signature_moments.moment: Truth is treated less as proof than as a narrative machine: connect the past, explain the present, predict the future.
signature_moments.source_phrase: connects the past ... explains the present ... predicts the future
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This gives the whole episode its operating test and lets Jiang proceed with a theory he repeatedly calls speculative.

signature_moments.moment: Eschatology becomes the operating system of secret power: the story of the end lets leaders coordinate after the founders die.
signature_moments.source_phrase: how do you ensure that the new leaders will coordinate together?
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns religious end-times belief into a governance mechanism rather than mere doctrine.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's method for testing a narrative by whether it connects past, explains present, and predicts future without claiming certain causation.
An account of how the world ends or paradise on earth is created; used here as a coordination script for secret societies.
The speculative alliance Jiang names as Jesuits, Jews, Frankists, Catholics, Freemasons, and Illuminati.

chronology_notes.note: This 2025-12-18 final Secret History lecture sets the method and speculative frame for the Pax Judaica model.

uncertainty_notes.note: Jiang explicitly marks the whole secret-society connection as speculative and says evidence is limited.
ASR damages several proper names and phrases around Adam Weishaupt and Illuminati legends, but the argumentative role is clear.
